marketName: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
coreJobStatement: Deliver continuous, secure, and compliant compute, storage, and network capacity to tenants and end users at contracted uptime and performance levels
productJobStatement: Help Emergency Exit Door Monitoring to detect, log, and alert on every emergency exit door activation with a tamper-evident, power-independent record -- enabling real-time SOC notification and SOC 2 CC6.4 / ISO 27001 A.7.2 audit evidence generation without mains wiring at each door point
lPath: L6d Physical Security & Access Control > L5 Emergency Exit Door Monitoring > L4 Door Guardian Alarm Unit

segments.name: Compliance-audit DCs
segments.characteristics: Tier III/IV facilities undergoing annual SOC 2 Type II and/or ISO 27001 recertification
segments.targetabilityReason: Every door event must produce tamper-evident digital evidence; audit evidence quality drives purchase decision
segments.circumstance: Tier III/IV facilities undergoing annual SOC 2 Type II and/or ISO 27001 recertification
segments.alternativesDiffer: Audit evidence quality drives purchase decision; alternatives differ radically vs. non-certified DCs

segments.name: Retrofit / legacy DCs
segments.characteristics: Existing facilities (pre-2015 build) without conduit to secondary emergency exit doors
segments.targetabilityReason: Wiring each door is prohibitively expensive or physically impossible; wireless is the only viable approach
segments.circumstance: Existing facilities (pre-2015 build) without conduit to secondary emergency exit doors
segments.alternativesDiffer: Wireless is the only viable approach; creates entirely different alternative set

segments.name: High-security / government cloud DCs
segments.characteristics: DCs hosting classified, defense, or critical-infrastructure workloads under FedRAMP, FISMA, or NIS2
segments.targetabilityReason: Physical security requirements are prescriptive and non-discretionary; any monitoring gap triggers regulatory action
segments.circumstance: DCs hosting classified, defense, or critical-infrastructure workloads under FedRAMP, FISMA, or NIS2
segments.alternativesDiffer: Physical security requirements are prescriptive; any monitoring gap triggers regulatory action, not merely a finding

segments.name: Distributed edge DC operators
segments.characteristics: Operators managing 5-50 micro-DCs with <=2 on-site staff per location
segments.targetabilityReason: Remote monitoring is not optional; no security guard to physically observe exits; silent battery failure = total monitoring loss
segments.circumstance: Operators managing 5-50 micro-DCs with <=2 on-site staff per location
segments.alternativesDiffer: Remote monitoring is not optional; no on-site guard to observe exits

segments.name: Hyperscale cloud (self-operated)
segments.characteristics: Operators with >=50,000 m2 single-campus facilities, owning the entire physical plant
segments.targetabilityReason: Scale demands standardization; high-density GPU racks create RF-hostile environments; highest-volume deployment scenario, cost-per-unit critical
segments.circumstance: Operators with >=50,000 m2 single-campus facilities, owning the entire physical plant
segments.alternativesDiffer: Scale demands standardization; RF-hostile environments from GPU racks; cost-per-unit critical

alternatives.name: Wired electronic exit alarm (panel-integrated) -- ~50% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: Requires conduit per door; high retrofit cost in legacy DCs; IP-native via panel but mains-dependent unless on dedicated UPS circuit; single panel failure = coverage loss across entire zone

alternatives.name: Wireless IoT door alarm guard (Turkwachter category) -- ~30%
alternatives.tradeoffs: No wiring; battery fail-safe; mechanical alarm independent of power; proprietary radio protocol requires IP gateway for PACS/SIEM; RF attenuation in metal-dense server halls; surface-mount only

alternatives.name: Mechanical-only door prop / handle alarm -- ~20%
alternatives.tradeoffs: Lowest cost; zero installation complexity; zero remote alerting; zero digital audit trail; cannot satisfy SOC 2 CC6.4 digital logging requirement -- eliminated by compliance mandate

alternatives.name: Door contact sensor + PACS integration (IP-native)
alternatives.tradeoffs: IP-native from day one; license-per-door cost; no physical guard/locking function; can be bypassed by physical force without guaranteed alarm; no mechanical fail-safe

alternatives.name: Do nothing / manual inspection rounds
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero capex; zero audit trail; guard log does not satisfy SOC 2 digital evidence standard; most common competitor because switching cost appears high and compliance risk appears low until audit

jobSteps.description: Define monitoring perimeter, alert thresholds, and SIEM log retention policy for all in-scope emergency exits per SOC 2 CC6.4 and ISO 27001 A.7.2
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Scope definition directly determines which doors are monitored and what events are logged -- foundational to compliance certification
jobSteps.rawStatement: Define monitoring perimeter (all in-scope emergency exits), alert thresholds, SIEM log retention policy per SOC 2 CC6.4
jobSteps.rationale: Scope definition directly determines which doors are monitored and what events are logged -- foundational to compliance certification

jobSteps.description: Survey all emergency exit doors; map RF propagation conditions through server hall; identify receiver and IP gateway placement positions
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: RF planning is a mandatory pre-installation step unique to wireless door monitoring in metal-dense DC environments
jobSteps.rawStatement: Survey all emergency exit doors; map RF propagation through server hall; identify receiver and IP gateway placement
jobSteps.rationale: RF planning is a mandatory pre-installation step unique to wireless door monitoring in metal-dense DC environments

jobSteps.description: Install door guardian units; pair with receivers; configure IP gateway; integrate event stream with PACS and SIEM platforms
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Installation and SIEM integration are the primary deployment tasks determining end-to-end chain-of-custody integrity
jobSteps.rawStatement: Install door guardian units; pair with receivers; configure IP gateway; integrate event stream with PACS and SIEM
jobSteps.rationale: Installation and SIEM integration are the primary deployment tasks determining end-to-end chain-of-custody integrity

jobSteps.description: Verify all units paired and alarm-to-SIEM chain functional; conduct per-door end-to-end activation test before go-live
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: End-to-end verification is required before SOC 2 monitoring evidence clock starts; unverified units create silent gaps
jobSteps.rawStatement: Verify all units paired and alarm-to-SIEM chain functional; conduct end-to-end activation test per door
jobSteps.rationale: End-to-end verification is required before SOC 2 monitoring evidence clock starts; unverified units create silent gaps

jobSteps.description: Detect and log door events in real time; generate SOC alerts; write tamper-evident timestamped records to SIEM
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Primary value-creation step -- the core job the product is hired to perform
jobSteps.rawStatement: Detect and log door events in real time; generate SOC alerts; write tamper-evident records to SIEM
jobSteps.rationale: Primary value-creation step -- the core job the product is hired to perform

jobSteps.description: Track battery depletion per unit; verify unit connectivity heartbeat; detect RF coverage drift caused by rack density changes
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Continuous monitoring of system health is required to prevent undetected coverage gaps -- a critical SOC 2 audit concern
jobSteps.rawStatement: Track battery depletion per unit; verify unit connectivity heartbeat; detect RF coverage drift with rack changes
jobSteps.rationale: Continuous monitoring of system health is required to prevent undetected coverage gaps -- a critical SOC 2 audit concern

jobSteps.description: Add/remove doors from monitoring scope; change alert thresholds; adapt to facility layout changes without full reinstallation
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Data centers continuously expand and reconfigure; monitoring scope must track facility changes without coverage gaps
jobSteps.rawStatement: Add/remove doors from monitoring scope; change alert thresholds; adapt to facility layout changes
jobSteps.rationale: Data centers continuously expand and reconfigure; monitoring scope must track facility changes without coverage gaps

jobSteps.description: Generate SOC 2 / ISO 27001 A.7.2 audit evidence per door with per-event granularity; compile compliance report for auditor
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Audit evidence generation is a direct compliance deliverable; quality of evidence determines certification outcome
jobSteps.rawStatement: Generate SOC 2 / ISO 27001 A.7.2 audit evidence per door; compile compliance report for auditor
jobSteps.rationale: Audit evidence generation is a direct compliance deliverable; quality of evidence determines certification outcome

stakeholders.who: DC Operations Director / Chief Compliance Officer

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit doors being excluded from the SOC 2 CC6.4 monitoring perimeter due to incomplete door inventory at deployment, resulting in >0 unmonitored exits in audit scope
Minimize the likelihood of alert threshold misconfiguration at deployment resulting in >5% false positive door alarm events per week, degrading Security Operations Center alert response discipline
Increase the ability to complete RF coverage planning and gateway placement for a 50-door deployment using a standard site survey toolkit in <=1 day of engineer effort
Minimize the likelihood of receiver placement errors causing >5% of installed door guardian units to report intermittent connectivity loss in a server hall with dense metal rack infrastructure
Increase the ability to install and commission a door guardian unit on any standard commercial exit door within <=15 minutes without specialist low-voltage contractor skills
Minimize the likelihood of incorrect pairing between door guardian radio transmitter and IP gateway causing alarm events to be silently dropped during initial deployment verification
Increase the ability to verify end-to-end alarm event propagation from each door guardian to the SIEM log within <=30 seconds of a manual test activation across all 50+ installed units simultaneously
Minimize the likelihood of door guardian units appearing as online in the gateway dashboard but failing to transmit alarm events under Faraday cage RF conditions, creating undetected monitoring gaps

Minimize the likelihood of an emergency exit door activation going unlogged in the SIEM audit trail during a facility power event -- including UPS switchover, generator start sequence, and mains failure
Increase the ability to deliver door activation alerts to the Security Operations Center console within <=30 seconds of physical door trigger under standard server hall RF conditions
Minimize the likelihood of an emergency exit door activation -- including authorized key override bypass -- going unlogged in the SIEM event record, permitting 0 unlogged activations per audit period
Minimize the variability in SIEM timestamp accuracy between physical door guardian activation and the corresponding log entry to <=5 seconds across all deployed units
Increase the ability to predict door guardian battery replacement needs with >=14 days advance warning per unit, enabling scheduled maintenance without emergency callouts
Minimize the likelihood of a door guardian unit going offline without automated SOC notification, creating a silent monitoring gap exceeding 15 minutes
Minimize the variability in wireless signal quality for door guardian units installed in server halls with variable rack density and AI/GPU thermal load shifts that alter electromagnetic propagation
Increase the ability to update door guardian monitoring configuration -- add/remove doors, change alert thresholds -- without on-site field work per door, completing a 10-door change in <=1 hour
Minimize the time to restore full door monitoring coverage to 100% of in-scope exits following a gateway or receiver failure, maintaining SOC 2 audit continuity within <=4 hours mean recovery time

Increase the ability to generate a SOC 2 Type II or ISO 27001 A.7.2 compliance report covering all door activation events across a 12-month audit period with per-door, per-event granularity in <=30 minutes
Minimize the likelihood of audit evidence rejection due to chain-of-custody gaps between the door guardian physical sensor event and the corresponding SIEM log entry under SOC 2 Type II scrutiny
Increase the ability to demonstrate an unbroken audit chain from physical door sensor event to SIEM log entry with zero evidence gaps across a 12-month SOC 2 audit period
Increase the ability to present complete emergency exit monitoring coverage to SOC 2 Type II auditors via a single API-queryable evidence endpoint without manual evidence compilation
Minimize the likelihood that security staff discover an unlogged or delayed emergency exit activation during a post-incident investigation that cannot be reconstructed in the audit record
